2606.29901.title: Semi-Supervised Sound Event Detection with Conditional Mixup and Embedding-Level Contrastive Loss
2606.29901.paper_summary: Sound event detection (SED) is a core module for acoustic environmental analysis, yet its performance is often limited by scarce labeled data. Recent systems leverage large pretrained audio foundation models, but effective fine-tuning remains challenging because labeled data are limited while unlabeled data are abundant. A previous work, ATST-SED, addressed this problem with a pseudo-label based semi-supervised fine-tuning framework. In this work, we further improve the framework by adopting an embedding-level self-supervised contrastive loss inspired by ATST-Frame pretraining. This contrastive objective better exploits unlabeled data during fine-tuning. One challenge is that mixup serves different roles in the two objectives: pseudo-label learning uses composition mixup, while contrastive learning treats mixup as a perturbation. To resolve this mismatch, we propose conditional mixup, which combines composition mixup and perturbation mixup in one semi-supervised framework and defines the corresponding embedding-level contrastive losses. The resulting model achieves 0.645 PSDS1 and 0.822 PSDS2 on the DESED validation set, establishing a new state of the art.
2606.29901.paper_summary_zh: 聲音事件檢測 (SED) 是聲學環境分析的核心模組，但其性能常常受到標記數據稀缺的限制。最近的系統利用大型預訓練的音頻基礎模型，但有效的微調仍然具有挑戰性，因為標記數據有限而未標記數據豐富。先前的工作 ATST-SED 通過基於偽標籤的半監督微調框架解決了這個問題。在本研究中，我們通過採用受 ATST-Frame 預訓練啟發的嵌入級自監督對比損失進一步改進了該框架。這一對比目標在微調過程中更好地利用了未標記數據。一個挑戰是，mixup 在這兩個目標中扮演著不同的角色：偽標籤學習使用組合 mixup，而對比學習將 mixup 視為擾動。為了解決這一不匹配，我們提出了條件 mixup，將組合 mixup 和擾動 mixup 結合在一個半監督框架中，並定義相應的嵌入級對比損失。最終模型在 DESED 驗證集上達到了 0.645 PSDS1 和 0.822 PSDS2，創造了新的技術標杆。
2606.29901.author: Nian Shao et.al.
2606.29901.authors: Nian Shao, Xian Li, Xiaofei Li
